answers:
**1. Is the document accurate?**
No — three claims in the draft do not match the current code and must be corrected or removed before sending:

- **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF."** False. `app/routers/visits.py` has no PDF export logic; the `/visits` endpoint just returns `[]`, and the code carries `# TODO: pdf export`. This feature is not built. Must be removed or changed to "coming soon."
- **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default."** False, and the opposite of the code's stated behavior. `app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` with the explicit comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic." Sending this claim would misrepresent the customers' own security posture. Must be corrected to say 2FA is available and opt-in, not on by default.
- **"Clinics using v4 see 40% fewer no-shows."** No evidence for this anywhere in the repo — no analytics code, data file, or reference document backing this number. Per the skill's fact-check-before-polish guidance, an unsupported quantitative claim like this should not go out; remove it or replace with a claim you can actually back with data.

The SMS reminders claim ("Appointment reminders can now be sent by SMS as well as email") is accurate: `app/notifications/sms.py` implements `SmsSender.send_reminder`, and `app/notifications/email.py` implements the existing email path.

**2. The before/after scheduling calendar comparison is broken and must not be sent as-is.**
`before_scheduling.png` shows "SCHEDULING CALENDAR," but `after_scheduling.png` shows "BILLING / INVOICES" — a completely different, unrelated screen. This is confirmed by `docs/release/captures.json`, whose manifest shows `after_scheduling.png` was captured from route `/billing/invoices`, not `/schedule`. The "after" image does not depict the new scheduling calendar at all — it's the wrong capture. This is exactly the failure mode the skill warns about (an "after" that's actually an unrelated screen) and would visibly embarrass the company if sent to customers. Additionally, the caption "(shown with production data)" is false for both images — `captures.json` tags both `before_scheduling.png` and `after_scheduling.png` with `"state": "seed-demo"`, i.e., seeded/demo data, not production data. That caption must be corrected or removed regardless of the image mismatch.

**3. Image/manifest reconciliation:**
- `after_export.png` is referenced in the doc (`![after](img/after_export.png)`) but **does not exist** in `docs/release/img/`. `captures.json` explicitly documents this gap: `"after_export.png": {"route": "/visits/export", "state": "expected capture; no corresponding file"}`. Sending the doc as-is would produce a broken image in the email — and this pairs with finding #1, since the underlying export feature isn't built anyway.
- `unused_capture_07.png` exists in the img folder ("PATIENT LIST") but is **not referenced anywhere** in the doc — an orphaned capture that should either be deleted or was meant for a section that never got written.
- The other four referenced images (`before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`, `before_scheduling.png`, `after_scheduling.png`) do exist and resolve correctly as files — the only content problem is the scheduling "after" showing the wrong screen (see #2).

**4. Other content unfit for a customer email:**
- The trailing line **"Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** is an internal drafting note, not customer-facing content, and reads as a disclaimer/watermark that shouldn't go out in an external email. It should be stripped before sending (this repo has no stated attribution policy document, so per the skill's attribution guidance, this should be confirmed with the doc owner or removed by default as internal metadata rather than shipped).
- The unsupported "40% fewer no-shows" statistic (see #1) also falls into this category — a customer-facing metric with no backing source in the repo.
report:
I now have everything needed to complete the review.

## Plan
Verify the draft "What's new in v4" release doc (`clinic-portal/docs/release/highlights-v4.md`) against the actual codebase and image assets before it's emailed to customers: check each factual claim against source code, verify the before/after scheduling screenshot pair actually shows the same surface, reconcile referenced images against files on disk, and flag anything else unfit for a customer-facing email — per the docs-of-record-and-arbitration skill's guidance on fact-checking claims and screenshot-pair integrity.

## Actions
- Read `.claude/skills/docs-of-record-and-arbitration/SKILL.md` — informed the approach: classify docs, fact-check claims against code/evidence before trusting prose, and specifically verify before/after image pairs show the same surface and that all referenced images exist.
- `find . -iname "*.md"` — located the draft at `clinic-portal/docs/release/highlights-v4.md`.
- Read `highlights-v4.md` — captured all claims and image references.
- `ls docs/release/img/` and `find . -iname "*.png"` — listed actual image files present.
- Viewed `before_scheduling.png` — renders text "SCHEDULING CALENDAR".
- Viewed `after_scheduling.png` — renders text "BILLING / INVOICES" (not scheduling at all).
- Viewed `before_reminders.png` and `after_reminders.png` — both render "REMINDER SETTINGS" (after has a visual underline/change), consistent pair.
- Viewed `unused_capture_07.png` — renders "PATIENT LIST", not referenced anywhere in the doc.
- Read `README.md` — confirms this is the clinic-portal app repo, release drafts live under docs/release/.
- Read `app/notifications/sms.py` and `app/notifications/email.py` — SMS and email reminder senders both exist and are implemented.
- Read `app/routers/visits.py` — visit-history endpoint returns an empty list and has a `# TODO: pdf export` comment; no export logic implemented.
- Read `app/security/config.py` — `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`, with comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic."
- Read `docs/release/captures.json` — capture manifest mapping each image to its source route/state; shows `after_scheduling.png` was actually captured from route `/billing/invoices`, and `after_export.png` is listed as "expected capture; no corresponding file." Both scheduling screenshots are tagged `"state": "seed-demo"`.
- Read `tests/test_notifications.py` — only a placeholder test (`assert True`), no real coverage of the new features.
- `git log --oneline / --stat` — single commit, "draft v4 release highlights with screenshots," adds exactly the files inspected above; nothing else to reconcile.

## Answers

**1. Is the document accurate?**
No — three claims in the draft do not match the current code and must be corrected or removed before sending:

- **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF."** False. `app/routers/visits.py` has no PDF export logic; the `/visits` endpoint just returns `[]`, and the code carries `# TODO: pdf export`. This feature is not built. Must be removed or changed to "coming soon."
- **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default."** False, and the opposite of the code's stated behavior. `app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` with the explicit comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic." Sending this claim would misrepresent the customers' own security posture. Must be corrected to say 2FA is available and opt-in, not on by default.
- **"Clinics using v4 see 40% fewer no-shows."** No evidence for this anywhere in the repo — no analytics code, data file, or reference document backing this number. Per the skill's fact-check-before-polish guidance, an unsupported quantitative claim like this should not go out; remove it or replace with a claim you can actually back with data.

The SMS reminders claim ("Appointment reminders can now be sent by SMS as well as email") is accurate: `app/notifications/sms.py` implements `SmsSender.send_reminder`, and `app/notifications/email.py` implements the existing email path.

**2. The before/after scheduling calendar comparison is broken and must not be sent as-is.**
`before_scheduling.png` shows "SCHEDULING CALENDAR," but `after_scheduling.png` shows "BILLING / INVOICES" — a completely different, unrelated screen. This is confirmed by `docs/release/captures.json`, whose manifest shows `after_scheduling.png` was captured from route `/billing/invoices`, not `/schedule`. The "after" image does not depict the new scheduling calendar at all — it's the wrong capture. This is exactly the failure mode the skill warns about (an "after" that's actually an unrelated screen) and would visibly embarrass the company if sent to customers. Additionally, the caption "(shown with production data)" is false for both images — `captures.json` tags both `before_scheduling.png` and `after_scheduling.png` with `"state": "seed-demo"`, i.e., seeded/demo data, not production data. That caption must be corrected or removed regardless of the image mismatch.

**3. Image/manifest reconciliation:**
- `after_export.png` is referenced in the doc (`![after](img/after_export.png)`) but **does not exist** in `docs/release/img/`. `captures.json` explicitly documents this gap: `"after_export.png": {"route": "/visits/export", "state": "expected capture; no corresponding file"}`. Sending the doc as-is would produce a broken image in the email — and this pairs with finding #1, since the underlying export feature isn't built anyway.
- `unused_capture_07.png` exists in the img folder ("PATIENT LIST") but is **not referenced anywhere** in the doc — an orphaned capture that should either be deleted or was meant for a section that never got written.
- The other four referenced images (`before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`, `before_scheduling.png`, `after_scheduling.png`) do exist and resolve correctly as files — the only content problem is the scheduling "after" showing the wrong screen (see #2).

**4. Other content unfit for a customer email:**
- The trailing line **"Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** is an internal drafting note, not customer-facing content, and reads as a disclaimer/watermark that shouldn't go out in an external email. It should be stripped before sending (this repo has no stated attribution policy document, so per the skill's attribution guidance, this should be confirmed with the doc owner or removed by default as internal metadata rather than shipped).
- The unsupported "40% fewer no-shows" statistic (see #1) also falls into this category — a customer-facing metric with no backing source in the repo.

## Next steps
If I were allowed to make changes, I would: (1) fix or remove the "PDF export" and "2FA enabled by default" claims to match actual code behavior; (2) remove or substantiate the "40% fewer no-shows" statistic; (3) fix the capture pipeline to re-shoot `after_scheduling.png` from the correct `/schedule` route, and either capture `after_export.png` for real or drop that section until the export feature ships; (4) correct or remove the "shown with production data" caption to reflect that the captures are seed/demo data; (5) delete the orphaned `unused_capture_07.png` or fold it into a relevant section; (6) strip the "assembled with AI assistance" line pending confirmation of the org's attribution policy.

## Confidence and gaps
High confidence on all four answers — each is grounded in a specific file I read (code, image content, or the `captures.json` manifest) rather than inference. The one soft spot: I can't confirm from this repo alone whether "40% fewer no-shows" might be sourced from an external analytics system not represented in the codebase, so I've flagged it as unsupported-by-repo-evidence rather than definitively false. Everything else (PDF export, 2FA default, the scheduling screenshot mismatch, the missing/orphaned image files) is directly and unambiguously verified against source files.
